This classification places it among the flowering plants that are native to New Zealand. The family Apiaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Psammogeton involucratum specifically falls under the subfamily Apioideae. This subfamily encompasses a wide range of plants, including carrots, parsnips, and many other herbs and vegetables. The classification of Psammogeton involucratum helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and genetic makeup.
